diagrams.title: Base Class Pointer to Derived Objects
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates how a single base class pointer can reference objects of different derived class types at runtime, demonstrating the core concept of runtime polymorphism through pointers.
elements: Rectangle representing BaseClass with label 'Animal' and virtual method 'speak()'
Two derived class rectangles: 'Dog' and 'Cat' inheriting from Animal
Arrow pointing from Dog and Cat to Animal showing inheritance
Pointer variable 'Animal* ptr' displayed at the top
Three memory boxes showing ptr pointing to Dog object, then to Cat object, then to Animal object
Dotted arrows showing the pointer moving between objects
Callout boxes showing the actual method that gets called when ptr->speak() is invoked
diagrams.purpose: Explains how a base class pointer enables runtime determination of which derived class method to execute, the foundation of runtime polymorphism

diagrams.title: Virtual Table (vtable) Mechanism
diagrams.description: This diagram visualizes the internal mechanism of virtual function resolution through vtables, showing how C++ implements runtime polymorphism at the memory level.
elements: Three object memory layouts: Base object, Derived1 object, Derived2 object
In each object, a vptr (virtual pointer) arrow pointing to a vtable
Three vtable boxes, one for each class, containing function pointer entries
vtable for Base showing pointer to Base::method()
vtable for Derived1 showing pointer to Derived1::method()
vtable for Derived2 showing pointer to Derived2::method()
Colored arrows showing how basePtr->method() follows vptr to vtable then to actual function
Label showing 'Runtime Resolution' at the point of method call
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the vtable mechanism that enables runtime binding of virtual function calls through pointers

diagrams.title: Polymorphic Array of Base Pointers
diagrams.description: This diagram shows a practical scenario where an array of base class pointers holds references to various derived class objects, demonstrating polymorphism in collections.
elements: Array of Animal* pointers with 4 slots
Four derived class objects: Dog, Cat, Bird, Lion positioned below
Arrows from each array slot pointing to respective derived objects
Code snippet box showing array declaration and initialization
Loop diagram showing iteration through array calling polymorphic method
Output box showing different results: 'Woof!', 'Meow!', 'Tweet!', 'Roar!'
Highlighted section showing that same method call produces different behaviors
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how runtime polymorphism enables treating different derived objects uniformly through base pointers in collections, a common real-world use case
